question_data.Question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Is listening to customer feedback really critical to the success of your business? Henry Ford, a founder of the car industry, famously claimed, “If I had asked people what they wanted, they would have said faster horses.” It is true that we cannot innovate by simply doing whatever customers want. But it is only one side of the issue. A bigger problem is that, in most cases, customers themselves don’t know what they want. You can demonstrate this for yourself quite easily. Think about some of your favorite movies. Now try to explain why you like them. Most of us have preferences that we can’t explain in detail. Therefore, a successful business does not just ask customers what they want. It figures out what they want before they know it themselves.
question_data.Options:
① the importance of figuring out what customers want before they know it themselves
② how Henry Ford’s “faster horses” quote changed the car industry
③ why asking customers for feedback is the most reliable method of innovation
④ the role of movie preferences in understanding consumer psychology
⑤ the differences between listening to customer feedback and ignoring it
question_data.Explanation: ①이 정답입니다. 글의 핵심 메시지는 고객이 원하는 것만 따르는 것은 혁신의 한계가 있고, 고객 스스로도 자신의 욕구를 설명하지 못하는 경우가 많으며, 성공한 기업은 고객이 스스로 알기 전에 그 욕구를 먼저 파악한다는 것입니다. ②는 헨리 포드 명언의 산업 영향, ③는 지문이 정면 반박하는 주장, ④은 단순 예시, ⑤는 지문의 범위와 다릅니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: The “big-fish-little-pond effect” is a phenomenon that has a big impact on how people view their ability and performance. If a fish is in a small pond, it will feel relatively big because of its environment. Likewise, if you are in an environment where the people around you are low performing, you will feel more positive about your own abilities. However, if you are surrounded by people who are high achieving, your perception of your abilities will get worse. In a recent study, Professor Thomas Götz tested this phenomenon. He found that math students feel better about themselves when they are in classes with low achievers than when they are in classes with high achievers. In other words, being around high achievers can be emotionally stressful for other students.
question_data.Options:
① the influence of surrounding peers’ performance levels on one’s perception of personal ability
② why high-achieving students tend to develop stronger self-confidence over time
③ how Professor Götz’s research improved math education in German schools
④ the reasons small ponds offer better living conditions for fish than large ones
⑤ the benefits of placing low-achieving and high-achieving students in the same class
question_data.Explanation: ①이 정답입니다. 글 전체가 ‘큰 물고기-작은 연못 효과’를 설명하며, 저성과자 주변에서는 자기 능력 인식이 높아지고 고성과자 주변에서는 낙아지는 현상을 소개합니다. 괴츠 교수 연구가 이를 뒷받침합니다. ②는 지문 내용과 반대, ③는 연구 목적이 다름, ④은 비유를 문자 그대로 이해한 것, ⑤는 지문에 없는 내용입니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Most people are very busy and find it difficult to schedule time to exercise. But working out in the early morning solves this problem. The biggest benefit is that light exercise activates your sleepy brain, allowing you to concentrate better and making you feel refreshed. And since most people don’t do much in the early morning, exercising is unlikely to create a scheduling conflict. At first, it may be difficult to get used to waking up earlier. But if you make exercise part of your routine, it can add consistency to your life. While starting isn’t easy, you’ll thank yourself once you see the results.
question_data.Options:
① the benefits of early morning workouts for busy people in terms of focus, scheduling, and building a routine
② how long-distance runners can improve their performance through morning training sessions
③ why exercising before breakfast is harmful to people who have a busy lifestyle
④ the connection between reducing screen time in the morning and improving exercise habits
⑤ the role of gym memberships in helping busy people maintain a consistent workout schedule
question_data.Explanation: ①이 정답입니다. 글은 바쁜 사람들의 운동 시간 부족 문제를 이른 아침 운동으로 해결할 수 있다고 제안하며, 뇌 활성화·집중력 향상·상취함, 일정 충돌 감소, 루틴화를 통한 일관성이라는 장점을 차례로 제시합니다. ②은 장거리 달리기 선수에 관한 내용, ③는 지문과 반대 방향, ④는 스크린 타임과 무관, ⑤는 헬스장 회원권에 관한 내용으로 지문의 논지와 다릅니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: In Australia, the term “tall poppy syndrome” refers to the tendency to speak badly about people who are very successful. This kind of behavior can seriously hurt talented and creative people who haven’t done anything wrong. It is partially caused by the mistaken idea that there is a limited amount of success available. Therefore, if one person is too successful, then everyone else suffers. This, of course, is not true. To stop this kind of behavior, we must change the way we view success. How successful we are has no connection to how successful others are. We should support those who have done well, and we should expect their support when we succeed.
question_data.Options:
① the need to recognize that others’ success does not limit one’s own and to shift from criticism to support
② the historical development of the tall poppy syndrome in Australian workplaces
③ why highly talented people tend to be more critical of others’ achievements
④ how limiting the total amount of success available makes competition fairer
⑤ the role of public awards in motivating talented employees in large companies
question_data.Explanation: ①이 정답입니다. 글은 tall poppy syndrome의 원인(성공이 한정되어 있다는 잘못된 생각)을 지적하고, 타인의 성공이 자신의 성공과 무관하다는 인식 전환과 상호 지지를 제안합니다. ②은 호주 직장 문화의 역사적 발전, ③는 지문 내용과 반대, ④은 지문이 명시적으로 부정하는 주장, ⑤는 지문에 없는 내용입니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Earthquakes happen when parts of the earth’s crust push against each other and release powerful vibrations. Frost quakes are similar, but they are smaller and involve different forces of nature. The essential fact behind frost quakes is that water expands when it freezes. Therefore, when water gathers underground and the temperature suddenly falls, the ground expands because of the water inside it. This pressure cracks the ground, making vibrations and loud sounds. Frost quakes require special conditions. For example, there must be a large amount of water in the ground, and the temperature has to drop so quickly that the water freezes before it can escape.
question_data.Options:
① the mechanism and special conditions required for frost quakes to occur
② why frost quakes cause more structural damage to buildings than regular earthquakes
③ how scientists measure the intensity of vibrations produced by frost quakes
④ the differences in frequency between earthquakes and frost quakes in cold regions
⑤ the historical discovery of frost quakes in regions near the Arctic Circle
question_data.Explanation: ①이 정답입니다. 글은 서리 지진의 원리(물이 얼면 팝창 → 지면 규열·진동)와 발생 조건(지면 내 대량의 물 + 급격한 온도 하강)을 설명합니다. ②는 지문에 없는 피해 비교, ③는 측정 방법에 관한 것으로 지문과 다름, ④는 빈도 비교로 지문과 다름, ⑤는 역사적 발견에 관한 것으로 지문에 없는 내용입니다.
